request_body.records: array of record hierarchies; each record = attributes {type, referenceId} + field values + children nested as {RelationshipName: {records: [...]}}

failure.semantics: if any record fails the entire request fails and rolls back; no partial ids may be reported

sobject_tree.no_cross_references: tree records cannot reference records outside their own hierarchy; cross-record references require the composite resource

request_body.allOrNone: boolean; true rolls back the whole call on any subrequest failure (this writer's default: true)

compositeRequest.referenceId: starts with a letter or number; only letters, numbers, underscores; unique per call
compositeRequest.body: the sObject field map; cross-record references written as the literal string @{referenceId.id}

composite.reference_syntax: @{referenceId.FieldName}; for creates @{referenceId.id} carries the created id into a later subrequest

response.status: HTTP 200 whether or not subrequests fail — per-subrequest httpStatusCode is the only success signal

compositeResponse.body: create success: {id, success, errors}; failure: array of {errorCode, message(, fields)}

composite.all_or_none_rollback: rolled-back sibling subrequests report errorCode PROCESSING_HALTED; after a rollback the writer must report zero created ids
client_rules: validate subrequest count and referenceId syntax and reference targets BEFORE sending anything over the wire
a @{ref.id} reference must point at an earlier subrequest in the same call
the write is successful only if every compositeResponse entry has a 2xx httpStatusCode; an HTTP 200 top-level response with failed entries is a failure

error_envelope.client_rule: top-level non-2xx responses raise SalesforceApiError with status and decoded errors; the access token never appears in error text
